content:
# I Built a Full-Stack Todo App Without Any Framework — Here’s What I Learned
![Todo App](https://fojizen.vercel.app/fojizenportfolio.png)
Most developers reach for React or Vue the moment they start a new project. I did the opposite. I built a **full-stack, production-ready todo application** using nothing but vanilla JavaScript, Express.js, and PostgreSQL.
Here’s the full story — from a blank folder to a deployed PWA with email verification, brute-force protection, and a dark theme.
## Why No Framework?
Frameworks are great. But I wanted to:
- Understand **exactly** what happens under the hood
- Keep the bundle size at **zero**
- Prove that vanilla JS is still powerful in 2026
And honestly? It worked.
## Tech Stack
- **Frontend:** Vanilla HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript (ES5+)
- **Backend:** Express.js 5.x
- **Database:** PostgreSQL (Supabase)
- **Auth:** JWT + httpOnly cookies
- **Password:** bcryptjs (12 rounds, async)
- **Email:** Resend API
- **PWA:** Service Worker + Web App Manifest
- **Deployment:** OnRender (backend)
## The Features That Matter
### Task Management
- Create, edit, delete tasks
- Priority levels (low, medium, high)
- Due dates with overdue highlighting
- Categories: Work, Personal, Shopping, Health, Education
- Tag system (comma-separated)
- Drag & drop reordering
- Filtering and sorting
- Bulk delete completed tasks
### User System
- Registration with username availability check
- **Email verification** (more on this below)
- Login with button spinner + checkmark animation
- JWT authentication with httpOnly cookies (7-day expiry)
- Secure password storage with bcrypt
### Design
- Dark / Light / System theme
- Particle animated background (Canvas API)
- Glassmorphism effects
- Modern loader animation
- Full responsive design
- TR / EN language support (i18n)
## Security: What Most Tutorials Skip
This is where I spent the most time. Here’s what I implemented:
### Rate Limiting
```
Login: 15 requests/minute
Register: 10 requests/15 minutes
Task CRUD: 30 requests/minute
```
### Brute Force Protection
5 failed login attempts → 15 minute lockout. Stored in the database.
### Security Headers
```javascript
app.use((req, res, next) => {
res.setHeader(‘Strict-Transport-Security’, ‘max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains’);
res.setHeader(‘X-Content-Type-Options’, ‘nosniff’);
res.setHeader(‘X-Frame-Options’, ‘DENY’);
res.setHeader(‘X-XSS-Protection’, ‘1; mode=block’);
res.setHeader(‘Referrer-Policy’, ‘strict-origin-when-cross-origin’);
next();
});
```
### Other
- CORS restriction (whitelist allowed origins)
- Input validation (type, length, regex, 500 char limit)
- SQL injection prevention (prepared statements)
- XSS protection (HTML encoding)
- Double-submit prevention (POST-only, 1s cooldown)
- Race condition prevention (ON CONFLICT)
## Email Verification Without SMTP
Here’s the twist: **OnRender blocks IPv6**. So `nodemailer` with Gmail SMTP? Dead on arrival.
```
Error: connect ENETUNREACH 2607:f8b0:4004:c0b::6d:465
```
**Solution:** [Resend API](https://resend.com) — pure HTTPS, no IPv6 needed.
```javascript
const sendVerificationEmail = async (email, token) => {
await fetch(‘https://api.resend.com/emails', {
method: ‘POST’,
headers: {
‘Authorization’: `Bearer ${RESEND_API_KEY}`,
‘Content-Type’: ‘application/json’
},
body: JSON.stringify({
from: ‘onboarding@resend.dev’,
to: email,
subject: ‘fojizen | TodoApp — Verify Your Email’,
html: verificationTemplate(token)
})
});
};
```
Free tier: 3,000 emails/month. More than enough.
## PWA: Installable Without a Framework
Yes, you can build a PWA with vanilla JS.
### manifest.json
```json
{
“name”: “fojizen TodoApp”,
“short_name”: “TodoApp”,
“start_url”: “/”,
“display”: “standalone”,
“theme_color”: “#7c5cff”,
“icons”: [
{ “src”: “/icon-192.png”, “sizes”: “192x192”, “type”: “image/png” },
{ “src”: “/icon-512.png”, “sizes”: “512x512”, “type”: “image/png” }
]
}
```
### Service Worker (sw.js)
- Network-first for HTML, JS files
- Cache-first for static assets
- Offline support built in
```javascript
self.addEventListener(‘fetch’, event => {
if (event.request.url.includes(‘/api’)) return;
event.respondWith(
caches.match(event.request).then(cached => {
return cached || fetch(event.request).then(response => {
const clone = response.clone();
caches.open(‘v1’).then(cache => cache.put(event.request, clone));
return response;
});
})
);
});
```
## PostgreSQL Gotchas
If you’re coming from SQLite, PostgreSQL will surprise you:
1. **`NOW()` must be cast:** `NOW()::text` for TEXT columns
2. **Booleans:** Use `true`/`false`, not `0`/`1`
3. **Quoted identifiers:** Needed for camelCase column names
```sql
— Wrong (SQLite style)
INSERT INTO users (createdAt) VALUES (NOW());
— Correct (PostgreSQL)
INSERT INTO users (createdAt) VALUES (NOW()::text);
```
## What I Learned
1. **Vanilla JS is enough** for most web apps
2. **Security takes time** — but it’s worth every minute
3. **PostgreSQL > SQLite** for production
4. **Resend API** saved me from SMTP headaches
5. **PWA is accessible** to everyone, not just React devs
## Live Demo & Source Code
- **Live:** [fojizen-todo-app.onrender.com](https://fojizen-todo-app.onrender.com)
- **GitHub:** [github.com/fojizen/todo-app](https://github.com/fojizen/todo-app)
- **Portfolio:** [fojizen.vercel.app](https://fojizen.vercel.app)
## What’s Next?
- Real-time collaboration
- Calendar integration
- Mobile app (React Native)
